message:
refactor(gateway): Migrate agent service to class-based auto-checkpoint API

This commit completes the migration from the legacy auto-checkpoint.ts
module (functional exports) to the new auto-checkpoint.service.ts
(class-based service with per-agent instances).

Background:
- The original auto-checkpoint.ts used module-level state and exported
  functions like initializeAutoCheckpoint(), stopAutoCheckpoint(), and
  onAgentMessage()
- The new auto-checkpoint.service.ts provides AutoCheckpointService
  class instances per agent, accessed via getAutoCheckpointService()
- This refactoring enables better testability, clearer ownership, and
  proper lifecycle management

Changes to agent.ts:
- Import getAutoCheckpointService() and removeAutoCheckpointService()
  from auto-checkpoint.service.ts instead of old module
- Import createErrorCheckpoint() from checkpoint.ts (was re-exported
  by old module)
- In spawnAgent(): Replace initializeAutoCheckpoint() with:
  - getAutoCheckpointService(agentId) to get/create service instance
  - setStateProvider() to configure state access callback
  - start() to begin auto-checkpoint monitoring
- In handleAgentEvents() termination: Replace stopAutoCheckpoint()
  with removeAutoCheckpointService()
- In terminateAgent(): Same replacement for cleanup
- In sendMessage(): Replace onAgentMessage() with
  getAutoCheckpointService(agentId).onMessage()

Deleted File:
- apps/gateway/src/services/auto-checkpoint.ts is removed as all
  consumers have migrated to the new service

The new API provides explicit lifecycle control:
- getAutoCheckpointService(id) - Get or create service for agent
- removeAutoCheckpointService(id) - Clean up when agent terminates
- service.setStateProvider(fn) - Configure how to fetch agent state
- service.start() - Begin monitoring
- service.onMessage() - Notify of message activity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
